Title: AtRH57, a DEAD-box RNA helicase, is involved in feedback inhibition of glucose-mediated abscisic acid accumulation during seedling development and additively affects pre-ribosomal RNA processing with high glucose

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: mediates sugar-induced growth arrest during germination and early seedling development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class II DEAD-box RNA helicase; DExD group; DESD motif; bipartite NLS
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: feedback inhibition of ABA accumulation; negative regulation of ABA biosynthesis/signaling gene expression downstream of HXK1 and promoted by ABI4
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: high glucose; ABA; salt; HXK1 pathway; ABI4
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ABA2; ABA3; AAO3; ABI4; ABI3; ABI5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: down-regulates ABA2, ABA3, and AAO3 under high glucose, thereby reducing ABA accumulation; inhibits ABI4 expression; indirectly reduces ABI3 and ABI5 expression; AtRH57 itself is promoted by ABI4 and requires HXK1 for full induction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA biosynthesis feedback control in sugar-mediated signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent negative regulator that provides feedback inhibition of glucose-mediated ABA accumulation during early seedling development
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: feedback inhibition of ABA biosynthesis and ABA accumulation within sugar-mediated ABA signaling; reciprocal relationship with ABI4
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in all organs and seed tissues; induced by ABA, 4.5% glucose, and NaCl
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in all organs and seed tissues; induced by ABA, high glucose, and salt
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high glucose (3–6%, especially 4.5%); ABA; NaCl; fluridone rescue assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA mutants (rh57-1/rh57-2/rh57-3); complementation; qRT-PCR; ABA quantification; fluridone rescue; subcellular localization; in vitro RNA unwinding assay; polysome/rRNA processing analyses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of AtRH57 increases glucose-induced ABA accumulation and ABA hypersensitivity, causing stronger seedling growth arrest under high glucose
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Arabidopsis ATH1 GeneChip microarray data analysis for seed development expression; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mutation causes delayed germination, small/pale seedlings, severe growth arrest under high glucose, ribosome biogenesis defects, altered antibiotic responses, and reduced protein synthesis function
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: could be used to modulate sugar/ABA sensitivity during seedling establishment, but manipulation is likely constrained by strong pleiotropic defects in rRNA processing and growth
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although AtRH57 is not a transcription factor, the Results establish it as the central causal regulator of nutrient-related ABA accumulation under glucose. It also functions in pre-rRNA processing and small ribosomal subunit biogenesis, linking sugar signaling with translational capacity.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtRH57 is the core gene underlying the rh57 glucose-hypersensitive phenotype. 2. Loss of AtRH57 causes elevated ABA accumulation under high glucose and hypersensitivity to ABA during germination/seedling growth. 3. AtRH57 acts downstream of HXK1 and is promoted by ABI4, while feeding back negatively on ABA2, ABA3, AAO3 and ABI4 to restrain ABA accumulation. 4. AtRH57 encodes a nucleus/nucleolus-localized Class II DEAD-box RNA helicase with ATP-independent dsRNA unwinding activity. 5. AtRH57 mutation additively with high glucose impairs pre-rRNA processing and 40S ribosomal subunit formation, contributing to growth arrest.
